creator: fbsdbugs@codeslip.net
text:
Same problem here. Various solutions found on the internet (https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/29492), which suggest using `:TSUpdate` or `:TSUpdateSync`, do not seem to work for reasons I'm unable to determine. Stack trace is cut off and I'm not sure I could reproduce the error in a way that would pull the full trace. I can suppress the error by running `:lua vim.treesitter.stop()`, but that's not ideal.

Running :checkhealth reports:

   ...snip...
‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
vim.treesitter: require("vim.treesitter.health").check()

 - Nvim runtime ABI version: 14
   ...snip...
 - OK Parser: vimdoc               ABI: 14, path: /usr/local/lib/nvim/parser/vimdoc.so

So the parser is installed.

It looks like maybe the parser should be bumped as well? 3.0.0 was released two months ago and looks like it had a breaking change which might match this error.

https://github.com/neovim/tree-sitter-vimdoc/releases/tag/v3.0.0
